description: Per-song ground-truth observations extracted from PDF reading. Used by key-aware-flat-filter as cross-reference to mitigate risk of wrongly flattening chromatic chords. Filter applies ONLY when pipeline-detected key matches the GT key for that song. Updated incrementally as more PDFs are read.

field_definitions.key_fifths: Integer matching MusicXML <fifths>. -3 = Eb major, +2 = D major, etc. null = key not visible / not yet read.
field_definitions.expected_flat_letters: Root letters that should be flatted by default in this key when the PDF shows them without an explicit natural. E.g. Eb major => ['B','E','A'] (Bb, Eb, Ab).
field_definitions.chromatic_root_notes: Root letters that DO appear as naturals in the chord row despite the key signature (e.g. 'A' in Eb major as a chromatic mediant). The filter MUST NOT flatten these.
field_definitions.tempo_marking: Italian tempo word from the score for cross-check.
field_definitions.lyrics_present: Whether the PDF contains lyrics (false for piano-solo arrangements like Promenade).

03 - I Got Plenty O' Nuttin'.comment: Sharp key (control). One Bb appears in the coda as a chromatic chord — keep it natural-Bb (root-alter -1) when seen but don't apply the filter.

04 - It Ain't Necessarily So.comment: F major / Gm. Many chromatic roots in the chord row (Db7, Eb7, F#m, A7, Gb). Filter only flattens B->Bb when natural is missing.

11 - Slap That Bass.comment: Eb major. Chord row dominated by Ebm/Gb/Abm/Bb7/Cb/Db. A few chromatic naturals appear: B7 / B9-7 / Cb (= B), F#m, D-stem chords. Filter must verify explicit natural sign before declining to flatten.

13 - Let's Call The Whole Thing Off.comment: Sharp key (control). No flat-glyph blindness expected. Stacked 9-over-7 (G⁹⁷, F#⁹⁷) appears — verified working through VLM blob-scan.

24 - Love Walked In.comment: Eb major. Verse modulates briefly: Bb7, Am7, D7, Gmaj7 (= D-major land) before returning. Many naturals are intentional. Filter for B/E/A only when no natural sign AND chord is in primary key area.

27 - They Can't Take That Away From Me.comment: Eb major. Verse uses E natural-dim, B7 and F7 chromatic dominants. Bridge to D7 area. Filter applies to remaining bare A/B/E roots.

02 - Bess You Is My Woman.comment: F major opening then modulates to D major mid-song, then to F#/B major for the duet finale. Many root letters appear as naturals because of the modulations. The score's primary <fifths> drives the filter so cross-key sections are out of scope.

05 - My Man's Gone Now.comment: D-minor centred recitative aria. Source PDF has NO chord-row labels printed at all. Pipeline correctly produces near-zero chord-symbols here; filter is moot.

17 - A Foggy Day.comment: F major. Chord row uses many Bb-rooted chords already flatted in the PDF (Bbmaj7, Bbm6, Bb7, Bb6) plus chromatic Db+, Eb7, Ebm6, E7, F#dim. Filter targets B->Bb when pipeline lost the flat.

08 - There's A Boat Dat's Leavin' Soon F.comment: Source PDF has NO chord-row labels printed (blues feel with chromatic piano voicings throughout). Pipeline correctly produces near-zero chord-symbols; filter is moot.

23 - Love Is Here To Stay.comment: F major. Chord row uses Bb explicit + many chromatic naturals (D7, A7, Dm, Eb9 in PDF). Filter targets B->Bb where the pipeline lost the flat; chromatic letters left alone.

09 - A Woman Is A Sometime Thing.comment: Bb major (G minor centred). Chord row uses many Eb-rooted chords explicit in the PDF (Eb, Eb7, Ebmaj7) plus Bb7, B+, C#dim. Chromatic naturals on A, C, D, F. Filter targets B and E where pipeline lost the flat.

15 - Shall We Dance.key_name: F major (verse opens in C major)
15 - Shall We Dance.comment: Two key areas: verse in C major (chord row uses natural-letter chords C, Em, F6, G7, B7, F#7sus4), modulates up by semitone into F-major refrain (Bb6, Bbmaj7, Eb7, Abmaj7, Db7, Gb explicit). Audiveris detected the song as F major. Filter targets B->Bb in refrain; chromatic list is broad to keep the verse natural chords intact.

06 - Oh Bess, Oh Where's My Bess.comment: Source PDF has NO chord-row labels printed. Pipeline correctly produces near-zero chord-symbols here; filter is moot.

19 - The Jolly Tar and The Milk Maid.comment: F major. Chord row dominated by Am/D/E7/A/A6 (chromatic naturals diatonic to A minor area) + Bb6 explicit + Db, Db+ chromatic dominants. Filter targets B->Bb where the pipeline lost the flat; keeps the many natural letter chords intact.

26 - For You, For Me, For Evermore.comment: Strongly Eb-major centric. Chord row mostly diatonic (Eb, Ab, Bb, Cm, Fm, Gm) with chromatic G/G+/G7 and F7/F9 plus Db. Filter on B/E/A applies broadly; chromatic_root_notes is narrow because the song stays close to Eb-major centre.

28 - By Strauss.comment: F major waltz. Verse centred on Am with many natural-letter chords (Dm6, E7, Am, Cm6, D7, C, G7). Refrain explicit flats: Bb, Bbm, Bbm6, Db, Db7, Eb7, Abm. Filter targets B->Bb where pipeline lost the flat; chromatic_root_notes broad to preserve verse natural letters.

29 - Sophia.comment: C major (no flats / no sharps). Chord row uses C, C7, G7, Dm7, Em, Em7, Cmaj7, F + chromatic Ab7, Bb, Db, Eb (all explicit flats in the PDF). Filter is a no-op because expected_flat_letters is empty in C major.

01 - The Real American Folk Song.key_name: unknown (Audiveris did not detect a stable key signature)
01 - The Real American Folk Song.comment: Audiveris reports no <fifths> for this score. Chord row on p1 includes A, Fm6, C, Cmaj7, Am7, Cdim suggesting C-major / A-minor territory. Filter is a no-op (key_match cannot be satisfied without a score key).

07 - Summertime.comment: Sharp key. Famously A-minor centred but notated with one sharp. Filter is a no-op because expected_flat_letters is empty.

10 - Just Another Rhumba.comment: Sharp key. Chord row includes A, Em, A9, G, Gdim, D7, D7+ — diatonic to G plus secondary dominants. Filter is a no-op.

12 - (I've Got) Beginner's Luck.comment: Sharp key (control). Filter is a no-op.

14 - Promenade (Piano Solo).comment: Piano solo arrangement, no lyrics and no chord-row labels expected. Filter is moot.

16 - They All Laughed.comment: Sharp key. Chord row uses G-major diatonic plus stacked alterations like A7(b9). Filter is a no-op.

18 - I Can't Be Bothered Now.comment: Sharp key. Chord row mixes diatonic (D7, Gmaj7, G6, A+) with chromatic Bb7, Bb, F, Eb on the way to bridge. Filter is a no-op because expected_flat_letters is empty in a sharp key.

20 - Nice Work If You Can Get It.comment: Sharp key. Filter is a no-op.

21 - I Love To Rhyme.comment: Sharp key. Filter is a no-op.

22 - I Was Doing All Right.comment: Sharp key. Filter is a no-op.

25 - The Back Bay Polka.key_name: unknown (Audiveris did not detect a stable key signature)
25 - The Back Bay Polka.comment: Audiveris reports no <fifths>. Chord row on p1 shows C, G7 in the refrain — C-major territory. Filter is a no-op (key_match cannot be satisfied without a score key).

30 - All The Livelong Day.comment: Sharp key. Filter is a no-op.
